response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise with 15+ years of experience and solid fundamentals in testing and software craftsmanship, this role requires a very different specialization. The most critical blocker is the missing Japanese language requirement (native-level proficiency explicitly required). Beyond language, the candidate's background is predominantly web-focused (recent 10 years in full-stack TypeScript/Node.js development) with minimal embedded systems or network security expertise. The role demands deep knowledge of LAN-switch firmware, network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel modification, and embedded C programming—areas where the candidate has limited or no demonstrated experience.
strengths: 15+ years of proven software development with strong fundamentals in testing, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship practices
Demonstrated Linux and systems-level expertise through infrastructure work (Docker, CI servers, git setup) and embedded projects
Adaptable across multiple programming languages and domains, showing career versatility
concerns: Does not meet the explicit requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency (not mentioned in resume)
Recent decade of work is web-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React); minimal embedded systems or firmware development background
No demonstrated expertise in network security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, SNMP, Telemetry) or LAN-switch architecture—core technical requirements

key_factors.factor: Transferable systems knowledge (Linux, C/C++, software practices)
